business.headline: Stock market crash: Why you should not stop your mutual fund SIPs in panicWhen markets fall sharply, fear often takes over investing decisions. But did you know continuing SIPs during market crashes has historically rewarded disciplined investors?

Centre procures more LPG, blames misinformation for panic-buyingIndia's domestic LPG production has increased by 25% following government orders to boost output, officials said at a joint briefing by the Ministry of Shipping, Ministry of External Affairs, and Ministry of Petroleum.

Japan first G7 nation to announce release of oil reserves amid Gulf conflictThe move comes as global energy markets remain on edge due to escalating tensions linked to the conflict involving Iran, which has raised fears of disruptions to crude oil shipments from the Middle East.

Energy body IEA agrees to release of record oil reserves amid Middle East crisis32 member countries of the International Energy Agency agreed on a coordinated release of oil from global strategic reserves as tensions linked to the Iran war raise fears of supply disruptions.

Dalal Street in panic: Sensex crashes 1,300 points, Nifty falls below 23,900The BSE Sensex closed 1,342 points lower, while the Nifty 50 slipped below the key 23,900 level by the end of the trading session.

Will costlier oil weaken the rupee and widen India's trade deficit?India consumes roughly 5.3 to 5.5 million barrels of crude oil every day. However, domestic production is only about 0.6 million barrels per day. This means the country depends on imports for nearly 85% of its oil needs.

Sensex crashes 1,300 points: Why is the stock market falling today?Sensex and Nifty fell sharply after opening higher as banking and auto stocks dragged the market down. So what triggered the sudden sell-off, and what should investors watch now?

Highlights: Domestic households given priority as LPG supplies are rationed, says Bharat PetroleumLPG Shortage Live Highlights: Concerns over a shortage of commercial LPG cylinders have surfaced across several Indian cities, with restaurants and hotels reporting disruptions in gas supply over the past few days. Hospitality associations warn that eateries in cities such as Mumbai, Bengaluru and Kolkata are struggling to procure commercial cylinders, forcing some to cut menus or scale down operations. The government, however, says there is no nationwide LPG crisis and has ordered refineries to increase production, with output raised by about 10% to stabilise supplies. The supply concerns come amid global energy market disruptions linked to tensions in West Asia, which have affected fuel logistics and availability. Track this live blog for the latest updates on the LPG cylinder shortage, government response, city-wise impact and supply situation across India.

India eases FDI rules for neighbouring countries: What the new 10% rule meansThe decision was approved by the Union Cabinet and the changes are intended to boost foreign investment, help startups access global funding and support manufacturing growth in India.

Why Reliance Industries shares are gaining in early trade todayAt around 9:23 a.m., RIL shares were trading at Rs 1,427.20, up Rs 18.40 or 1.31% from the previous close of Rs 1,408.80. The stock opened at Rs 1,424.90 and touched an intraday high of Rs 1,434.00.

Sensex, Nifty trade flat in early session; Reliance gains, banks weighAt around 9:18 a.m., the BSE Sensex was at 78,279.66, up 73.68 points or 0.09%. The Nifty 50 stood at 24,256.90, down 4.70 points or 0.02% after touching an intraday high of 24,292.35.

Air India fares to rise as Iran war pushes up jet fuel pricesAir India said the fuel surcharge is due to the sharp rise in aviation turbine fuel (ATF) prices since early March 2026, triggered by supply disruptions in the Gulf region.

G7 stops short of releasing oil reserves, asks IEA to assess market optionsAfter soaring to a more than three-year high in the previous session, oil prices plummeted 7 per cent on Tuesday as US President Donald Trump predicted the war in the Middle East could end soon, easing concerns about prolonged disruptions to oil supplies.

India eases FDI rules for China, allows up to 10% stake without govt approvalUnder the revised policy, overseas investors with up to 10% beneficial ownership from countries sharing a land border with India will now be allowed to invest through the automatic route.

Main Hoon Naa: IndiGo interim head's message to employees amid leadership changeRahul Bhatia expressed gratitude to employees who worked tirelessly to restore operations during that challenging period, praising their resilience and dedication.

IndiGo CEO Pieter Elbers steps down 3 months after operational meltdownThe resignation comes after the airline faced its worst-ever operational crisis in December, which drew scrutiny from both the public and the government.

Airlines raise fares as Iran conflict sends jet fuel prices soaringThe Iran-US conflict has disrupted shipping through a key global oil export route, pushing oil prices higher. As a result, airline tickets on some routes have become much more expensive, and there are growing concerns that travel demand could weaken if the situation continues.

No LPG crisis, production up 10% today, says government, warns against fake newsGovernment sources said oil refineries have increased LPG production by around 10% following directions issued earlier and that additional administrative measures have been put in place to prevent hoarding and malpractice.

Middle East countries cut daily oil output amid Hormuz disruptionFour of the region's largest producers, including Saudi Arabia, Iraq, the United Arab Emirates and Kuwait have collectively reduced output by as much as 6.7 million barrels per day.
Sensex closes 640 points higher, Nifty above 24,200; M&M gains 4%The S&P BSE Sensex jumped 639.82 points to close at 78,205.98, while the NSE Nifty50 added 233.55 points to end at 24,261.60.

entertainment.headline: Tisca Chopra pays tribute to mother Pammi Arora: Those who knew her loved her Actor Tisca Chopra shared an emotional tribute on Instagram after the death of her mother, Pammi Arora. She remembered her mother's joyful nature, life lessons, and her legacy as a beloved teacher.

FA9LA singer Flipperachi cancelled shows in India days before gig. Here's why Bahraini rapper Flipperachi became a household name in India after his viral hit FA9LA from the film Dhurandhar. However, he will not perform at scheduled shows in Bengaluru and Mumbai. Read to know the reason.

Allu Arjun's Allu Cinemas: India's biggest Dolby Cinema screen in Hyderabad Allu Arjun's Allu Cinemas, located inside Allu Studios in Kokapet, will begin public operations this week with major releases including Dhurandhar: The Revenge and Ustaad Bhagat Singh playing on India's biggest Dolby Cinema screens.

Anand Deverakonda shares chaotic-but-happy dance video with Vijay, Rashmika. Watch Actor Anand Deverakonda shared a joyful dance video featuring Vijay Deverakonda and Rashmika Mandanna from their wedding celebrations. The clip shows the celebrity couple dancing with their friends and Anand to the song Sancharame from the film Epic.

Kritika Kamra, Gaurav Kapur are now married: Newlyweds pose for media. See pics Actor Kritika Kamra and cricket commentator Gaurav Kapur got married in a private signing ceremony in Mumbai on March 11. Actors Farhan Akhtar, Malaika Arora, Neha Dhupia, Soha Ali Khan, among others were in attendance.

So damn cool: Masaba Gupta gushes as dad Viv Richards named Commonwealth Envoy Masaba Gupta shared a video of her father, legendary cricketer Vivian Richards, following his appointment as a Commonwealth envoy. She expressed her immense pride, while gushing over how "cool" he looked in his new role.

Radikaa Sarathkumar urges Chief Minister MK Stalin to make Thaai Kizhavi tax-free Veteran actor Radikaa Sarathkumar, at the success meet of her latest film Thaai Kizhavi, urged Tamil Nadu Chief Minister MK Stalin and his son, Deputy Chief Minister Udhayanidhi Stalin, to grant the film tax-free status.

Did Gaurav Khanna receive Rs 50 lakh for winning Bigg Boss 19? Read what he said Gaurav Khanna has revealed if he is yet to receive the Rs 50-lakh prize money after winning Bigg Boss Season 19 in December 2025. Read to find out.

Here's why Ashish Chanchlani chose Ekaki release on YouTube, not OTT In an exclusive interview with India Today, Ashish Chachlani spoke about his new sci-fi comedy show Ekaki. He even reflected on his decision to release the show on YouTube instead of OTT.

Karnataka film body warns paps after Sapthami Gowda flags objectionable filming The Karnataka Film Chamber of Commerce (KFCC) issued a strong warning to paparazzi, digital media, and content creators after concerns were raised about the way female actors are being filmed at public events, following complaints from Sapthami Gowda and other industry members.

Maamla Legal Hai Season 2 trailer: Ravi Kishan's VD Tyagi takes the judge's chair The trailer for Maamla Legal Hai Season 2 offers a glimpse into new cases and developments at Patparganj District Court. The upcoming season will see returning cast members alongside new additions, with the show set for an April 3 premiere on Netflix.

Ravi Mohan reacts to criticism amid divorce case: Men are the biggest victims Actor Ravi Mohan responded to criticism over his marital issues, sharing a message on social media about his perspective on men's experiences in marital conflicts.

Charithra Chandran of One Piece Season 2 is proud to be brown and Indian Charithra Chandran speaks exclusively to India Today about the significance of her casting as a win for South Asian visibility, sharing her journey as a proud Indian actor in a global franchise.

Mona Singh says she was 'mindblown' by Gaurav Gera's transformation in Dhurandhar Mona Singh expressed pride over her Jassi Jaissi Koi Nahin co-star Gaurav Gera receiving widespread praise for his role as Aalam Bhai in Dhurandhar.

Dhurandhar's Rakesh Bedi reacts on Toxic being postponed due to US-Iran conflict As Dhurandhar: The Revenge gets ready for release on March 19, actor Rakesh Bedi, who returns as Jameel Jamali, notes that the film has not been postponed by the US-Iran conflict, unlike Toxic. He also addresses reports that suggest his character is based on Pakistani politician Nabil Gabol.

'Stop with this brain rot': Sona Mohapatra slams Badshah's song Tateeree amid row Singer Sona Mohapatra called out Badshah's latest song, Tateeree, for reinforcing harmful stereotypes and objectifying women. Following widespread criticism, the song was taken off all platforms.

Director Harish Shankar on Ustaad Bhagat Singh vs Dhurandhar 2: No competition As Ranveer Singh's Dhurandhar: The Revenge and Pawan Kalyan's Ustaad Bhagat Singh are set to release on the same date, director Harish Shankar has reacted to the box office clash.

Daniel Radcliffe asks for mandatory therapy for children on film sets Daniel Radcliffe said therapy should be mandatory for child actors on film sets. Hinted that the upcoming Harry Potter series may introduce stronger mental health support.

Prithviraj Sukumaran, Manju Warrier to star in Rahul Sadasivan's horror film: Report Malayalam filmmaker Rahul Sadasivan, known for acclaimed horror titles like Bhoothakaalam and Bramayugam, is reportedly preparing another supernatural drama. The film reportedly features Prithviraj Sukumaran and Manju Warrier.

general news.headline: Shot fired at wedding attended by Farooq Abdullah, man points gun at ex-J&K CMShots were fired at a wedding in Jammu in the presence of former Chief Minister Farooq Abdullah and serving Deputy Chief Minister Surinder Choudhary, the police said. The accused was overpowered by the crowd present there and taken into custody by the police later.

Live: Trump claims US knocked out Iran leadership twice, says oil prices matter of warUS-Israel-Iran War Live Updates: The conflict between Iran and the joint front of Israel and the United States entered its 12th day on Wednesday, with fresh Iranian missile attacks targeting central Israel before dawn as air raid sirens sounded, and Israeli air defences intercepted incoming rockets. Iranian state media also reported new strikes on US military installations in Bahrain early Wednesday, following a wave of Iranian missile and drone attacks late Tuesday on bases linked to the US in Qatar, Iraq's Kurdistan, the UAE and Bahrain. The escalation comes after intense US and Israeli airstrikes on Iran on Tuesday, described by the Pentagon and witnesses as among the heaviest of the war so far. Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps warned it could block oil shipments from the Gulf unless the attacks stop, raising fears of disruption to global energy supplies. Meanwhile, the White House reiterated US President Donald Trump's warning of severe retaliation if Iran attempts to halt shipping through the Strait of Hormuz, even as he said US forces had destroyed 10 Iranian 'inactive' mine-laying vessels in recent strikes. Follow IndiaToday.in for live updates on the Middle East war.

My father had a very close shave: Omar Abdullah on 'point-blank' shooting attemptReacting to the incident in a post on X, Omar Abdullah said the details of the episode were still unclear but confirmed that a man armed with a loaded pistol managed to get dangerously close to his father.

US behind deadly strike on Iran school, probe finds usage of outdated data: NYTA US military investigation has determined that officers used outdated data provided by the Defense Intelligence Agency during the initial days of the Iran war and the strike was likely a targeting mistake, The New York Times reported.

Iran war: God is great. So is the other God. Also, the other oneThe current war in the Middle East isn't being fought for oil or money or power. All the parties involved, Iran, the US and Israel, are utterly convinced that it is a divine task and the Almighty Himself is on their side.

US strikes Iran, South Korea learns a hard lesson about alliancesSouth Korea had put a lot at stake for cooperating with the US on the THAAD project in 2016 – from the wrath of China, Seoul's largest trading partner, to protests from its own citizens. Now, ten years later, one may wonder if the gamble was too big a political investment.

Deplore endangering civilian crew: India after Gujarat-bound ship hit in HormuzA cargo vessel bound for Gujarat caught fire after being hit by an unidentified projectile in the Strait of Hormuz, prompting an Omani navy rescue that evacuated most of the crew.

Induction stoves vanish from stores as India grapples with LPG crisisA shortage in LPG cylinders across India has triggered a sharp surge in demand for induction cooktops. Quick commerce platforms and retailers in several neighbourhoods across Mumbai, Delhi and Bengaluru are running out of stock. Retailers report selling 50 to 70 units of induction stoves a day amid panic-buying.
Kumbh fame Monalisa defies family, marries Muslim boyfriend under cop protectionThe couple tied the knot at a temple in Kerala after police said both were adults and free to decide their future.

Get ready for $200 a barrel: Iran warns the world as oil on the boil Live Updates The war launched by Israel and the US against Iran continues to escalate, with missiles, drones, and naval attacks being used to disrupt shipping in the Strait of Hormuz, which is a small waterway and carries one-fifth of the world's oil supply.

Winks, kisses, low attendance in Parliament: Amit Shah shreds Rahul GandhiWhile defending the Lok Sabha speaker during his speech on the no-trust motion, Amit Shah launched a scathing attack against the Leader of the Opposition for his alleged low attendance in Parliament and took a jibe at his foreign trips and actions like winking eyes and blowing flying kisses.

Feel the lowest ever: Dentist dies by suicide, blames boyfriend in 6-page noteStuti Sonawne detailed in the suicide note how her boyfriend, Faizul Mohammad Khan, constantly insulted her and used derogatory remarks. She wrote that his constant disrespectful behaviour had made her feel the "lowest ever" in life.

Kritika Kamra, Gaurav Kapur are now married: Newlyweds pose for media. See picsActor Kritika Kamra and cricket commentator Gaurav Kapur got married in a private signing ceremony in Mumbai on March 11. Actors Farhan Akhtar, Malaika Arora, Neha Dhupia, Soha Ali Khan, among others were in attendance.

Opposition's no-trust motion against Speaker defeated in Lok Sabha amid uproarAfter a charged debate and protests in the House, the Lok Sabha rejected the opposition's no-confidence motion against Speaker Om Birla by voice vote.

India's high-protein 'designer rice' could help fight diabetesScientists in India have developed a new type of rice that contains three times more protein and has a lower glycaemic index. The innovation could help tackle diabetes, malnutrition, and nutrient deficiencies without changing people's eating habits.

2 meals a day, no dosa-poori: Bengaluru PG body's guidelines amid LPG crunchBengaluru PG accommodations have begun restricting meal options and limiting gas-heavy dishes like dosa, poori and chapati due to a shortage of commercial LPG cylinders. PG owners have advised switching to rice-based dishes, reducing weekday meals to two, and using electric cooking alternatives.

FA9LA singer Flipperachi cancelled shows in India days before gig. Here's whyBahraini rapper Flipperachi became a household name in India after his viral hit FA9LA from the film Dhurandhar. However, he will not perform at scheduled shows in Bengaluru and Mumbai. Read to know the reason.

IPL to begin with RCB vs SRH in Bengaluru as BCCI unveils first-phase scheduleIPL 2026: The BCCI announced the schedule for the first 16 days of the 19th season of the IPL. Defending champions RCB will take on SunRisers Hyderabad in Bengaluru on March 28 in the opening game.

Energy body IEA agrees to release of record oil reserves amid Middle East crisis32 member countries of the International Energy Agency agreed on a coordinated release of oil from global strategic reserves as tensions linked to the Iran war raise fears of supply disruptions.

New US WISA bill may ease strict H-1B rules, major relief likely for IndiansA new US proposal called the WISA bill could roll back strict rules imposed on the H-1B visa programme in 2025. If passed, the legislation may ease hiring barriers for companies and benefit thousands of Indian professionals and students who rely on the H-1B pathway to build careers in the United States.

This IIT Delhi wind tunnel turns Bernoulli's Principle into a breezeThe 'Wind Tunnel', yes, you read that right, at IIT Delhi is often referred to by students as a real-life example of Bernoulli's Principle, a concept they usually study in physics textbooks.

India now has over 50 CheetahsThe birth marks a proud moment for Project Cheetah, which aims to restore the world's fastest land animal to Indian landscapes decades after it went extinct in the country.

Trump announces historic $300 billion oil refinery in Texas, thanks RelianceAnnouncing the project in a Truth Social post, Trump said the refinery will be built in Brownsville, Texas, as part of a USD 300 billion deal aimed at boosting American energy production and strengthening economic ties with international partners.

Actor Hansika Motwani, businessman husband Sohael Khaturiya granted divorceActor Hansika Motwani and businessman Sohael Khaturiya have ended their four-year marriage. A family court in Mumbai has granted the couple a divorce.

sports.headline: Pakistan cricketers find no takers in Hundred Women 2026 auction No Pakistani players were sold in the Women's Hundred auction, with Fatima Sana and Sadia Iqbal shortlisted but left unsold. Attention now shifts to the men's auction, where 14 Pakistani players, including Haris Rauf, Shaheen Afridi, and Shadab Khan, are set to feature.

IPL 2026 unlikely to face oil, LPG disruptions, BCCI ready with contingency plans The upcoming Indian Premier League 2026 (IPL 2026) season is unlikely to be affected by the ongoing oil and LPG crisis in the country, amid attacks on Iran launched by the USA and Israel.

Gujarat Titans appoint Vijay Dahiya as assistant coach ahead of IPL 2026 Gujarat Titans (GT) have appointed former India wicketkeeper batter Vijay Dahiya as their assistant coach ahead of the Indian Premier League 2026 (IPL 2026).

Richa Ghosh, Smriti Mandhana reunite at Manchester, Deepti Sharma joins Sunrisers Richa Ghosh joined Manchester Originals Women in The Hundred Women's auction, reuniting with Smriti Mandhana, while Deepti Sharma was picked by Sunrisers Leeds Women. Meanwhile, Yastika Bhatia and N Shree Charani went unsold.

No regrets about T20 World Cup snub, spent time with dying father: Jitesh Sharma Speaking in the lead-up to IPL 2026, Jitesh Sharma said he was disheartened after being left out of India's T20 World Cup squad. However, the RCB wicketkeeper-batter added that he has no regrets, as the snub allowed him to spend precious time with his father, who died on February 1.

Arshdeep Singh didn't need to say sorry to Daryl Mitchell: Gautam Gambhir Gautam Gambhir said Arshdeep Singh did not need to apologise to Daryl Mitchell after their on-field exchange in the ICC Men's T20 World Cup final. Arshdeep was later fined 15 per cent of his match fee by the ICC for breaching the Code of Conduct.

Iran says it won't take part in FIFA World Cup hours after Trump's green light The FIFA World Cup has been plunged into a geopolitical firestorm. Just hours after FIFA President Gianni Infantino announced that US President Donald Trump had guaranteed safe entry and a warm welcome for the Iranian national team, Tehran has officially declined to participate.

After T20 World Cup win, Gautam Gambhir reveals his next big goal for Team India India head coach Gautam Gambhir has set his sights on qualifying for the World Test Championship (WTC) final in 2027 after guiding his team to back-to-back ICC white-ball triumphs.

Shaheen Afridi retains faith in young Pakistan brigade despite Mirpur hammering BAN vs PAK: Shaheen Shah Afridi said he will not lose faith in the young players despite Pakistan's eight-wicket defeat to Bangladesh in the ODI series opener on Wednesday at the Sher-e-Bangla National Cricket Stadium.

IPL to begin with RCB vs SRH in Bengaluru as BCCI unveils first-phase schedule IPL 2026: The BCCI announced the schedule for the first 16 days of the 19th season of the IPL. Defending champions RCB will take on SunRisers Hyderabad in Bengaluru on March 28 in the opening game.

Ex-selector says Gautam Gambhir ignores him completely, recalls Dhoni-era fallout Former chief selector Sandeep Patil reveals Gautam Gambhir has refused to speak to him since being dropped in 2013, recalling a long-standing rift that began during MS Dhoni's captaincy tenure.

IPL 2026 schedule Highlights: RCB to open campaign vs SRH at Chinnaswamy on March 28IPL 2026 Schedule Announcement Highlights: The Royal Challengers Bengaluru will face Sunrisers Hyderabad to open their season as defending champions at the M Chinnaswamy Stadium on March 28.

Dhoni never asked to drop Yuvraj Singh: Ex-BCCI selector sets record straightEx-BCCI chief selector Sandeep Patil has gone on record to say that MS Dhoni never once asked for Yuvraj Singh to be dropped from the Indian team, contradicting Yograj Singh's long-held claims.

No Babar Azam and Pakistan slip to new low in first match after T20 World CupPAK vs BAN, 1st ODI: Without Babar Azam in their lineup, Pakistan slumped to their lowest ODI total against Bangladesh when the two teams met on Wednesday, March 11 at the Sher-e-Bangla National Stadium in Mirpur. Bangladesh chased the target with 209 deliveries remaining.

ICC awards Rs 24 crore prize money to India for T20 World Cup 2026 triumphThe International Cricket Council (ICC) has announced Rs 24 crore prize money for the Indian cricket team for winning the T20 World Cup 2026. India beat New Zealand in the final to clinch their third title.

New Zealand Women spinner goes past Viv Richards to cap off dream seriesNZW vs ZIMW: Amelia Kerr enjoyed a dream ODI series against Zimbabwe, taking 16 wickets from three matches at an economy rate of 4.07. Earlier in the limited-overs leg of the tour, the New Zealand skipper also claimed a seven-wicket haul and scored her maiden T20I century.

Suryakumar, Shivam Dube felicitated by Maharashtra CM Fadnavis for T20 World Cup winT20 World Cup 2026-winning captain Suryakumar Yadav and Shivam Dube were felicitated by Maharashtra CM Devendra Fadnavis at Varsha Bungalow in Mumbai.

MS Dhoni will play full IPL 2026 season, can't talk about his role: CSK CEOIPL 2026: MS Dhoni, 44, will play the entirety of the upcoming season, Chennai Super Kings CEO Kasi Viswanathan said on Wednesday, March 11. The upcoming edition of the IPL is likely to get underway on March 28.

Will Bumrah focus more on white-ball cricket than Tests? Glenn McGrath reactsGlenn McGrath wonders if Jasprit Bumrah will continue playing Test cricket and will switch his focus to white-ball formats. Bumrah was the star of the show as India defeated New Zealand in the final to win the T20 World Cup.

Tell me what I can do? Surya reveals Samson's inspiring mid-World Cup questionT20 World Cup: India captain Suryakumar Yadav revealed that Sanju Samson had asked what he could do for the team despite starting the 2026 ICC Men's T20 World Cup on the bench. Samson later emerged as India's biggest hero of the tournament.

F1's return in India gathers pace as sports minister meets motorsports bodySports Minister Mansukh Mandaviya met with the FMSCI to discuss reviving Formula One in India, exploring a framework to build a pipeline of drivers and technicians.

Varun Chakravarthy dethroned as No.1 T20 bowler after poor World CupT20 World Cup: Varun Chakravarthy's reign at the top of the ICC T20I bowlers rankings has come to an end after his lacklustre performance during the second phase of the T20 World Cup 2026.

T20 World Cup: Abhishek Sharma, Ishan Kishan top ICC rankings, Samson makes big leapIshan Kishan has climbed to No.2 in the ICC T20I batter rankings, while Sanju Samson jumps 18 spots to 22nd with a career-best 637 points after India's T20 World Cup triumph.

Kirti Azad defends remarks on Team India's temple visit: Everyone should be respectedFormer India cricketer Kirti Azad has defended his criticism of Team India for taking the T20 World Cup 2026 trophy to a temple, as head coach Gautam Gambhir hit back at the remarks.

Donald Trump has given Iran green light to play FIFA World Cup in USA: InfantinoFIFA president Gianni Infantino said US President Donald Trump had assured that Iran would be allowed to travel to the United States to compete in the 2026 FIFA World Cup. The confirmation came amid growing doubts over Iran's participation due to rising geopolitical tensions.

Thought it was a dig at Ro-Ko: Ex-ENG star on Gambhir's T20 WC final commentsFormer England pacer Steve Harmison believes Gautam Gambhir's post-T20 World Cup remarks targeted senior Indian batters Rohit Sharma and Virat Kohli. Gambhir said he wants to create a culture in Indian cricket where the focus is on the team's success rather than personal milestones.

Virat Kohli's preparations begin, hits the nets ahead IPL 2026: WatchVirat Kohli has been spotted hitting the nets ahead of IPL 2026, with the Indian batting great putting in the hard yards early as defending champions RCB gear up for the new season.

Gambhir responds to MS Dhoni's viral post, explains why he doesn't smile in dugoutGautam Gambhir responded to MS Dhoni's viral smile remark after India's T20 World Cup win and explained why he chooses to keep a serious face during matches. Dhoni's special message to Gambhir after the T20 World Cup win went viral.

India's next T20 dynasty: Sanju Samson and Co carry Rohit-Kohli's legacy forwardTwo years after Rohit Sharma, Virat Kohli and Ravindra Jadeja stepped away from T20Is, India's new core delivered the perfect answer. Sanju Samson, Ishan Kishan, Axar Patel and Shivam Dube carried the legacy forward with a title-winning run at the 2026 T20 World Cup.

PSG president Nasser Al-Khelaifi stranded in Qatar amidst ongoing West Asia crisisPSG president Nasser Al-Khelaifi has been stranded in Qatar for over a week due to West Asia war flight disruptions, as the club prepares to host Chelsea in the Champions League.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
